backend/app/estimator/user_text_step.py.reason: FTY-281 crosses the 750-line source threshold wiring the comparable-reference aggregate fallback into UserTextMacroEstimator: a brand-dropped identity search for compatible reference pages, per-page compatibility/plausibility filtering, cold-pass page-fact extraction, and deterministic median aggregation (via comparable_reference.py) filling missing macros before the model-prior cold-pass; plus the review-round hardening — retained contributor provenance + read-model estimate_basis, sanitized search queries, and gated cold-pass product identity. +7 in the FTY-281 review round routing the exact reference lookup query through the same sanitized_identity + sanitize_query chokepoint as the comparable tier so prompt-like parser output in the item name never egresses. +16 in the FTY-281 review round failing closed on an empty sanitized identity in both the exact single-source lookup and the comparable brand-dropped search: with no surviving item-identity token the query degenerates to the broad fixed intent (``nutrition facts``) alone and the single-source path has no per-page compatibility gate, so it now returns early to the comparable/model-prior/unknown tiers instead of committing an unrelated page as a source-backed match. Additive; no schema/migration/serving-math change.

backend/tests/test_daily_summary_api.py.reason: Existing over-threshold first-party test file at FTY-200 baseline; +198 in FTY-223 for the uncounted-entries (needs_clarification events + proposed food items) coverage — uncounted-only day across single-day and range, finalized/empty zero cases, pending/processing/failed exclusion, per-day range density, and owner-scoping. +53 in FTY-280 covering a calorie-only user_text item counting its calories while unknown macros are skipped (not summed as 0).

backend/tests/test_parse_step.py.reason: Existing over-threshold first-party test file at FTY-200 baseline; +68 in FTY-280 covering user-stated nutrition extraction — the stated_* fields carried onto the candidate, a stated calorie total as a detail signal that avoids clarification, and a negative stated value failing closed as schema-invalid. +60 in the FTY-280 review round for the stated-calorie stability gate: contradictory duplicate totals across samples and a majority-missing total fail closed to a targeted calorie question, while a minority-missing sample does not over-clarify.

backend/tests/test_user_text_resolution..reason: FTY-281 crosses the 1000-line test threshold with the comparable-reference aggregate coverage: exact-source-miss -> compatible aggregate fill with source refs/assumptions, exact-source-wins -> no aggregation, incompatible/outlier rejection, insufficient-evidence fall-through to model-prior provenance, explicit user calorie/macro preservation, search-query sanitization, and the persistence/read-model provenance (rough comparable-reference label, contributor content hashes + per-100g snapshots, no raw page retention). +11 in the FTY-281 review round extending the sanitization test to assert instruction/personal-context tokens are dropped from every egressed query (exact + comparable), not just punctuation/control chars. +69 in the FTY-281 review round adding test_empty_sanitized_identity_fails_closed_without_a_broad_source_lookup, which drives the full parse/user_text pipeline with an item name that sanitizes to no identity token and asserts the resolver issues no broad source-backed lookup, leaves macros unknown, and asks no second serving question. +40 in the FTY-281 review round adding test_duplicate_source_hits_do_not_satisfy_the_minimum, which drives the pipeline with a provider returning the same reference URL three times and asserts the duplicate hits collapse to one distinct source so the macros stay unknown and no second serving question is asked. Additive test coverage only.

docs/contracts/evidence-retrieval.md.reason: FTY-280 implements FTY-279: +15 documenting the landed estimator/migration work (the user_text_step, the reference-search → model-prior cold-pass → unknown missing-macro order, and the additive 0018 migration adding basis + field_provenance and making the fact-snapshot columns nullable). Existing over-threshold first-party source document at FTY-200 baseline; +207 in FTY-279 (contract only) documenting user-stated nutrition text as first-class rank-1 `user_text` evidence — the source system + `user_text:<content_hash>` reference (raw phrase never stored), the `as_logged` fact basis (no per-100g lie), the optional per-field `field_provenance` map and unknown-vs-zero (nullable) macros, the fallback-rule precedence for stated fields, the missing-field estimation order (source-backed lookup → comparable-source aggregation as rough reference evidence with source refs + compatibility + plausibility/outlier filtering → model prior), bounded/plausibility validation, the worked Sobeys-wrap example, and the migration note. +15 in the FTY-279 review round contracting the FTY-158/FTY-159 cold-pass path for the missing-field model-prior step: an uncertain estimate uses N independent self-consistency passes scored against the calibrated operating point (never a one-shot confidence guess), and because a missing macro on an already-resolved `user_text` item is an optional estimate, pass disagreement leaves the field rough/unknown rather than triggering a second clarification about a detail the user already supplied. +24 in FTY-281 documenting the landed comparable-reference aggregation tier (comparable_reference.py wired into UserTextMacroEstimator between the single-source reference lookup and the model-prior cold-pass): the brand-dropped identity search, per-page compatibility check (food form/category + ingredient/flavor overlap), per-100g canonicalisation + plausibility gate, Atwater-space outlier rejection, median grams-per-kcal aggregation, minimum-source/material-disagreement fall-through, and the additive comparable_reference source_refs entry with no schema/migration/SourceType change. +8 in the FTY-281 review round documenting the retained contributor provenance (each reference_source with its content hash + per-100g fact snapshot in assumptions) and the additive optional ItemSourceDTO.estimate_basis read-model field distinguishing the rough comparable-reference aggregate from a plain user_text item. +3 in the FTY-281 review round documenting that the minimum-source gate counts DISTINCT sources — duplicate search hits sharing one reference_source:<url> collapse to a single source, so repeated hits of one page can never satisfy MIN_COMPARABLE_SOURCES. +3 in the FTY-281 review round noting the compatibility ingredient/flavor overlap must be a real food term — prompt-injection/chat-framing/personal-context words the raw diary phrase and an adversarial page happen to share are excluded, so a page with no shared food term can never read as compatible. +72 in FTY-298 documenting estimate_first rough-estimate provenance: recognizable identities and source/serving misses fall forward to reference/comparable/model/default-prior evidence, exact/reference/aggregate/model provenance stays distinguishable, raw text/output remains out of assumptions/source refs/logs, and no schema/code change is required. FTY-303 tightens the baseline after extracting duplicated global estimator policy wording to docs/contracts/estimator-policy.md. +20 in FTY-252 documenting count-serving named-food evidence (`serving_count`), count-ratio scaling, gram relation scaling, and incompatible-count fallback. +18 in FTY-253 documenting the bounded item-identity query-variant set the official/reference search consumer may send (name+brand base, quantity-phrase product-hint token orders, static retailer alias expansion) and the brand/product-compatibility check branded evidence candidates must pass — every variant still item identity only through the same sanitize_query chokepoint.

docs/contracts/food-resolution.md.reason: FTY-280 implements FTY-279: +11 documenting the landed user_text_step (UserTextResolveStep + UserTextMacroEstimator, wired before the food step) and the additive 0018 evidence_sources migration. FTY-278 (contract only) routes a mixed food log to the new first-class `partially_resolved` event status: crosses the 750-line source threshold documenting the item-scoped partial routing (mixed → `processing → partially_resolved` carrying committed `resolved` siblings; no-costable → event-level `needs_clarification`) across the version note, routing tables, and migration note. +129 in FTY-279 (contract only) adding the **User-Stated Resolution** section: direct resolution of a user-stated calorie total from `user_text` `as_logged` evidence (calories counted immediately, missing macros estimated via the source-backed-lookup → comparable-source-aggregation → model-prior order or left `null`), the no-second-follow-up clarification boundary (clarify only for a component with no usable detail or self-contradictory/implausible facts), the Sobeys-wrap worked example, and the version/routing/errors/migration notes. +3 in the FTY-279 review round narrowing the FDC **Candidate input** note: a candidate may now carry user-stated `stated_*` nutrition facts (routed to the `user_text` evidence path), while the FDC request itself still receives only the sanitized food name — reconciling the old "LLM never supplies nutrition facts" line with the new parser/evidence contract. +14 in FTY-281 documenting the comparable-reference aggregate fallback (UserTextMacroEstimator now searches brand-dropped identity for compatible references, filters compatibility/outliers, and fills missing macros from their median before the model-prior cold-pass; deterministic aggregation in comparable_reference.py; additive, no schema/migration/serving-math change). +3 in the FTY-281 review round noting the retained contributor content hash + per-100g snapshot in assumptions and the additive optional ItemSourceDTO.estimate_basis read-model field. +10 in FTY-292 documenting that explicit cracker count plus measured household-volume hummus is sufficient detail and exact source misses fall forward to rough provenance rather than re-asking for quantity. +108 in FTY-298 documenting the estimate_first food-resolution boundary, shared clarify-mode env contract, source/serving misses falling forward to rough provenance before questions, stricter self-hoster modes, barcode/source miss fallback semantics, and privacy-safe rough assumptions. FTY-303 tightens the baseline after extracting duplicated global estimator policy wording to docs/contracts/estimator-policy.md. +48 in FTY-252 documenting official_source/v2 count-serving facts, deterministic count-ratio scaling before default-serving fallback, model-prior count assumptions, and incompatible-count result rejection. +79 in FTY-253 documenting brand-aware packaged-product routing: the version entry, the Brand-aware packaged-product routing subsection (generic FDC hit as candidate-not-authority gated on brand/product compatibility + quantity-costability, bounded identity-variant search with static retailer alias expansion, per-candidate evidence gating, clarification as last resort), the updated routing rows, and the migration note.

mobile/components/SettingsScreen.test.ts.reason: Existing over-threshold first-party test file at FTY-200 baseline; +33 in FTY-186 for the native-segmented-control selectSegment/segmentValues helpers that drive units/appearance/cadence/goal segments via the platform onChange instead of per-pill onPress; +171 in FTY-267 for the visual-review seam coverage (goal_edit/body_edit/appearance sub-states opened via the E2E-only initial-state seam, plus the release/non-E2E inertness proof).

mobile/components/TrendsScreen.tsx.reason: FTY-265 adds the visual-review `weight.sheet` sub-state seam: registers the preset through the FTY-247 API, reads an E2E-only, isE2EMode()-gated initial value for the weight-log sheet's `sheetVisible` state, and computes the settled-marker testID (passed to WeightLogSheet, gated on the weight-entries read reaching "ready"), crossing the 750-line source threshold; +4 from merging main's FTY-264 side-effect import of components/trends/visualReviewPresets (registering the `trends.adherence_retry` preset) with its explanatory comment.
